mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding its 12-month moving average compresses equity risk premiums by confirming earnings expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in above-trend GDP quarters are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals tightening labor markets that boost consumer spending and corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns in months with >0.2pp UNRATE declines are not significantly positive relative to flat or rising UNRATE months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the federal funds rate and CPIAUCSL indicate disinflationary monetary easing that lowers discount rates without triggering stagflation fears, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative or zero predictive Rank IC over a 2-year out-of-sample period, or forward returns do not differ significantly from periods where only one series declines.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth above its 6-month average reflects capacity utilization recovery that lifts industrial and cyclical equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns in above-trend INDPRO months are not statistically higher than baseline over a 3-year walk-forward test, or predictive IC remains negative.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month cumulative change in total nonfarm payrolls confirms labor market resilience that supports aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in months with positive 3-month PAYEMS changes are not significantly higher than baseline (p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly housing starts exceeding 1.3 million annualized units signals robust construction demand and household formation, which historically precedes positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns in months with HOUST > 1.3M are not significantly positive relative to months below threshold, or predictive IC drops below 0.02 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rises in the federal funds rate and unemployment rate indicate policy tightening outpacing labor market strength, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative forward returns over a 3-year out-of-sample period, or forward returns in these periods are not significantly lower than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS alongside stable CPIAUCSL indicates healthy housing demand without inflationary pressure, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns in months with rising RSAFS and stable CPI are not significantly positive relative to control periods, or predictive IC remains below 0.01 over rolling windows.
